title: SOHO Design, Configuration, and Secure Administration · Incident Repair Bay
summary: A realistic soho design, configuration, and secure administration ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in secure maintainable SOHO network design and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate users, devices, applications, coverage, capacity, availability, privacy, accessibility, support, cost, and growth into a bounded topology.
Secure management identity, credentials, MFA where supported, firmware, time, backups, remote administration, logs, recovery, and physical access.
Separate trusted, guest, IoT, work, management, and exposed services with least-required communication and explicit exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Requirements Topology
steps.instruction:
debug case — A home business mixes work, family, guests, cameras, printers, IoT, backups, and remote access behind a default ISP gateway. The debug variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate users, devices, applications, coverage, capacity, availability, privacy, accessibility, support, cost, and growth into a bounded topology. Artifact under review: secure maintainable SOHO network design.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Requirements Topology, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Requirements Topology.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document ISP boundary, configuration identity, address plan, device inventory, backups, restore, reset, outage fallback, owner, and support escalation.
